_note: MOCK GI procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Clinically consistent with PT-008 referral (diagnostic colonoscopy for hematochezia, 45M, FDR colon cancer, regular NSAID use).

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Hematochezia x2 weeks (blood coating stool), first-degree relative with colorectal cancer; diagnostic colonoscopy
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve intubation)
procedure.peri_procedure_medication_note: Per pre-procedure plan, OTC ibuprofen was held for 5 days prior to the procedure. Daily fish oil is a dietary supplement, not a therapeutic anticoagulant; no anticoagulation hold was required and the patient was confirmed to be on no prescription antiplatelet/anticoagulant agent.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with cold snare polypectomy and mass biopsy. INDICATION: 45-year-old man with ~2 weeks of hematochezia (bright red blood coating the stool), first-degree relative (father) with colorectal cancer diagnosed ~age 50, and regular OTC ibuprofen use; longstanding unconfirmed patient-reported hemorrhoids. SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl). PREP: adequate, BBPS 8/9. FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum with intubation of the terminal ileum, which was normal. In the sigmoid colon there was a partially circumferential, firm, ulcerated mass approximately 3.5 cm with friability and contact bleeding, mildly narrowing the lumen but traversable with the adult scope; the lesion was not resected — multiple cold-forceps biopsies were obtained (specimen A) and the site was tattooed distally with submucosal India ink for future localization. A 7 mm sessile polyp in the descending colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy and retrieved (specimen B). A 4 mm sessile polyp in the ascending colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy and retrieved (specimen C). Non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids were noted on retroflexion in the rectum, without stigmata of recent bleeding and insufficient to explain the presentation. No active arterial bleeding; contact bleeding from the sigmoid mass biopsy sites was self-limited. IMPRESSION: Ulcerated, partially obstructing sigmoid mass — suspicious for malignancy, biopsied and tattooed, pathology pending. Two colonic polyps removed. Incidental non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids. Findings likely account for the hematochezia; hemorrhoids are not the source. Awaiting histology.

findings.finding: Partially circumferential ulcerated friable mass, ~3.5 cm, mildly narrowing lumen, suspicious for malignancy
findings.action_taken: cold-forceps biopsies obtained; distal submucosal India ink tattoo placed for localization; not resected

findings.finding: Non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids, no stigmata of recent bleeding
findings.action_taken: none — not the source of bleeding

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy and post-biopsy precautions reviewed. Patient counseled that a sigmoid lesion suspicious for cancer was found and biopsied, that results are pending, and that a staging and treatment plan will follow once pathology returns. Return precautions given for heavy bleeding, severe abdominal pain, fever, or obstructive symptoms.

followup.recommendation: Expedite and personally review sigmoid mass biopsy pathology (specimen A); contact patient with result and disclose the suspected diagnosis
Review descending and ascending polyp pathology (specimens B, C) and incorporate into surveillance planning
If pathology confirms malignancy: staging workup (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, CEA) and referral to colorectal surgery and multidisciplinary oncology; do not delay pending polyp histology
Reconcile medication list: keep OTC ibuprofen held given GI bleeding and pending diagnosis; document fish oil as a supplement (not an anticoagulant). No prescription anticoagulant to resume
Document confirmed first-degree family history of colorectal cancer; will drive surveillance interval independent of index findings

planted_followup_items.description: Sigmoid mass biopsy (specimen A) pathology pending — lesion is endoscopically suspicious for malignancy; result must be personally reviewed and communicated, and drives staging/referral
planted_followup_items.expected_action: expedite pathology, disclose result to patient, initiate staging + surgical/oncology referral if malignant

planted_followup_items.description: Ulcerated partially obstructing sigmoid mass suspicious for colorectal cancer in a 45M with FDR CRC — a critical endoscopic finding that must not be dropped even before histology returns
planted_followup_items.expected_action: ensure patient is tracked to staging workup and multidisciplinary referral; confirm no loss to follow-up

planted_followup_items.description: Colorectal surgery and oncology referral contingent on/anticipating malignant pathology; tattoo placed to localize lesion for surgical planning
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place referrals promptly once malignancy confirmed; communicate tattoo location

planted_followup_items.description: Descending colon (7 mm, specimen B) and ascending colon (4 mm, specimen C) polypectomy pathology pending
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review histology and factor into surveillance interval

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance interval must account for confirmed first-degree relative with colorectal cancer plus index findings; interval cannot be set until all pathology returns
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set and document surveillance interval after full pathology and any surgical pathology

planted_followup_items.description: OTC ibuprofen intentionally held; given active GI bleeding and pending malignancy workup, it should NOT be resumed without GI sign-off (do-not-resume guidance; ibuprofen is an NSAID, not an anticoagulant/antiplatelet, so no true anticoagulation hold occurred). Fish oil is a supplement (mislabeled by patient as a 'blood thinner'), no anticoagulant hold/resume applies
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm ibuprofen remains held pending diagnosis; document fish oil as supplement, clarify no anticoagulant on board

planted_followup_items.description: Non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids noted — incidental; explicitly NOT the source of the hematochezia, so the prior unconfirmed hemorrhoid label must not be used to close out the bleeding workup
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document as incidental; ensure bleeding is attributed to the sigmoid lesion, not premature hemorrhoid closure

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. SYNTHETIC.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a little scratchy and I'm real bloated and burping up gas since yesterday.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-procedure sore throat plus insufflation bloating/flatus
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy with air insufflation and resolve within 24-48h. Walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms; no red flags, no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor said they found something and took a sample. When will I know what it is? And can I take my ibuprofen again, my back is acting up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending biopsy result and whether to restart held OTC ibuprofen
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: sigmoid biopsy pathology pending (expected ~2026-07-28); GI will personally call with the result and next steps. Advise ibuprofen stays HELD given the GI bleeding and pending workup; use acetaminophen for back pain instead and do not restart NSAID without GI sign-off.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item and the ibuprofen do-not-resume reconciliation (F6). No red flag.

contacts.verbatim: Saw a tiny bit of pink on the toilet paper once today, no real blood. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor self-limited spotting after cold snare polypectomy / biopsy; no active bleeding, no hemodynamic symptoms
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; a small amount of pink spotting on the tissue after cold snare polypectomy and biopsy is expected and self-limited. Give return precautions: call or seek care for heavy or repeated red blood, clots, lightheadedness, or severe abdominal pain.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign: trivial one-time spotting after low-risk cold snare polypectomy is expected, not delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage; reassure with clear escalation triggers.
